lede: A look at January 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Konrad Adenauer and Clancy Brown, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Eris (dwarf planet)
events.desc: The dwarf planet Eris is discovered by Palomar Observatory-based astronomers, later motivating the International Astronomical Union (IAU) to define the term planet for the first time.

events.title: Georgia (country)
events.desc: Georgian forces enter Tskhinvali, the capital of South Ossetia, Georgia, opening the 1991–92 South Ossetia War.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: Gunmen shoot dead ten Protestant civilians after stopping their minibus at Kingsmill in County Armagh, Northern Ireland, UK, allegedly as retaliation for a string of attacks on Catholic civilians in the area by Loyalists, particularly the killing of six Catholics the night before.

events.title: 1970 Tonghai earthquake
events.desc: The 7.1 Mw Tonghai earthquake shakes Tonghai County, Yunnan province, China, with a maximum Mercalli intensity of X (Extreme). Between 10,000 and 15,000 people are known to have been killed and about another 26,000 are injured.

events.title: Cultural Revolution
events.desc: Cultural Revolution: The Shanghai People's Commune is established following the seizure of power from local city officials by revolutionaries.

events.title: Amy Johnson
events.desc: Amy Johnson, a 37-year-old pilot and the first woman to fly solo from London to Australia, disappears after bailing out of her plane over the River Thames, and is presumed dead.

events.title: Golden Gate Bridge
events.desc: Construction of the Golden Gate Bridge begins in San Francisco Bay.

events.title: Nellie Tayloe Ross
events.desc: Nellie Tayloe Ross of Wyoming becomes the first female governor in the United States.

events.title: German Workers' Party
events.desc: The German Workers' Party, which would become the Nazi Party, is founded in Munich.

events.title: Louis XV
events.desc: Louis XV of France survives an assassination attempt by Robert-François Damiens, who becomes the last person to be executed in France by drawing and quartering (the traditional form of capital punishment used for regicides).

births.name: Konrad Adenauer
births.line: German lawyer and politician, Chancellor of West Germany

births.name: Umberto Eco
births.line: Italian novelist, literary critic, and philosopher

deaths.text: Douglas Shearer, Canadian-American sound designer and engineer
Max Born, German physicist and mathematician, Nobel Prize laureate
Calvin Coolidge, American lawyer and politician, 30th President of the United States
Bolesław IV the Curly, High Duke of Poland

observances.text: Christian Feast day: Charles of Mount Argus
Harbin International Ice and Snow Sculpture Festival (Harbin, China)
